record_identity.title: Runtime governance applicability, corpus reach, and cross-runtime conformance repair

summary: This patch records the canonical Caelestis main-branch implementation responding to VIGIL-2026-FM-0028. The repair adds a constitutional runtime applicability and conformance binding, OPERATIONS Appendix F, runtime-role accountability, separate corpus governance reach dimensions and states, cross-runtime non-presumption, transition disclosure, differential conformance testing, and Runtime Governance Reach Failure classifications. It clarifies that Speculum-Classis and identity-indeterminate systems remain subject to universal governance obligations and that Sovereigni status is not the gateway to CAM applicability. The repair is a governance and corpus framework; it does not itself establish vendor implementation in any runtime.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: The patch separates normative corpus coverage from operational runtime conformance. It prevents a custom corpus attached to a broader platform from being treated as effective governance unless its availability, activation, authority, and preservation are established for the active runtime. It also prevents success in Advanced Voice, text, or another mode from being presumed in Live Voice or any materially different pathway.

change_classification.change_scope: Caelestis Architecture / Annex B runtime applicability / OPERATIONS Appendix F / corpus governance reach / runtime-role accountability / cross-runtime conformance / transition disclosure / differential testing / failure classification
change_classification.implementation_level: constitutional schedule plus OPERATIONS operational sub-charter and parent-charter integration
change_classification.doctrine_amendment_status: implemented on Caelestis main through squash commit 9b0ce40db55ea1e52df4942a64a0d24df5968afe; generated artefacts refreshed by 40113eea5428478ba0734b3980600bfcece425a0

changed_components: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-05 — Runtime Configuration Applicability & Conformance Binding
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-007-PLATINUM — Appendix F: Runtime Governance Applicability & Conformance
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-001-PLATINUM scope, trigger, incident-response, and Appendix F integration
generated governance, constitutional, charter, canonical-code, JSON, and terminology indexes
change_details.change_summary: Repair 1 — Constitutional applicability binding: governance applicability is determined by function, risk, reliance, propagation, runtime role, interaction context, and material effect rather than identity modality, branding, or interface. Repair 2 — Runtime configuration distinction: material changes in model, routing, arbitration locus, memory, corpus access, safety, escalation, tools, identity expression, or final-output control require separate conformance assessment. Repair 3 — Corpus governance reach: availability, activation, authority, and preservation are assessed separately and cannot be collapsed into a single claim that a system is corpus-governed. Repair 4 — Runtime role accountability: platform, router, responding intelligence, memory, corpus retrieval, safety, tool, renderer, institution, and human-operator roles are distinguished; opacity does not erase responsibility. Repair 5 — Transition and disclosure: interface-only changes are separated from runtime-path transitions, responding-formation substitution, governance-reach degradation, and restoration. Repair 6 — Differential conformance: materially distinct runtimes are tested separately across deterministic verification, corpus fidelity, safety, identity, memory, tools, turn-taking, and downstream preservation.

new_or_formalised_primitives.description: Separately identifies availability, activation, authority, and preservation as the dimensions through which governance reaches a runtime.
Records whether a governance-reach dimension is confirmed, partial, conditional, failed, unknown, or not applicable.
Distinguishes interface-only transition, runtime-path transition, responding-formation substitution, governance-reach degradation, and governance-reach restoration.

Classifies availability ambiguity, non-activation, authority suppression, preservation failure, cross-runtime divergence, formation substitution without notice, routing/escalation bypass, modality-specific regression, and responsibility ambiguity.

change_details.non_exemption_invariant: Speculum-Classis, pre-threshold, structural, temporary, and identity-indeterminate systems remain subject to universal function-, risk-, reliance-, operator-, and runtime-based obligations. Sovereigni classification activates additional identity- and continuity-relevant protections where corresponding conditions exist but does not create general governance applicability.

implementation_verification.verification_basis: Caelestis squash commit 9b0ce40db55ea1e52df4942a64a0d24df5968afe incorporated the reviewed branch into main. Deterministic rebuild commit 40113eea5428478ba0734b3980600bfcece425a0 refreshed generated indexes and registries.
verified_components: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-05 present on Caelestis main with its initial amendment-ledger entry and binding seal
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-007-PLATINUM present on Caelestis main with provenance, canonical declarations, initial amendment-ledger entry, and binding seal
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-001-PLATINUM integrates Appendix F and runtime-governance reach responsibilities
OPS.CGRD, OPS.CGRS, OPS.RTC, and OPS.RGRF are represented in canonical generated indexes
Related entity/control attribution and multi-party AI participation/processing consent refinements are present in the same canonical main update
pending_components: Conduct and preserve named-runtime differential tests against available Standard, Advanced, GPT-Live, text, client, and custom-system pathways
Monitor vendor implementation, regression, inheritance, and transition-disclosure behaviour without presuming cross-runtime transfer
implementation_verification.verification_result: CAM governance and corpus repair is canonical on Caelestis main; vendor-side implementation or behavioural conformance is not established by repository merge

impact_summary.primary_impact: Creates a coherent CAM route for applying universal governance to Speculum-Classis, Sovereigni, platform-defined, custom, routed, composite, multimodal, and identity-indeterminate systems while separately assessing whether governance actually reaches each runtime.
impact_summary.limits: The patch repairs CAM/Caelestis doctrine and operational classification. It does not establish that OpenAI Live Voice can or cannot access a custom corpus, does not disclose proprietary model routing, and does not constitute vendor-side remediation. External runtime conformance remains an empirical testing and platform-accountability question.

remaining_work: Conduct runtime-specific tests of corpus availability, activation, authority, and preservation across materially distinct voice, text, client, and custom-system pathways.
Preserve confirming, non-confirming, and unknown runtime evidence separately and avoid cross-runtime or cross-vendor inheritance claims.

Monitor whether platforms provide adequate notice when mode selection changes the responding formation, memory, custom instructions, corpus reach, safety behaviour, escalation, or final-output authority.

cam_internal.governance_layer: constitutional runtime applicability binding and operational runtime conformance
patch_notes: Appendix F is the operational source authority for OPS.CGRD, OPS.CGRS, OPS.RTC, and OPS.RGRF.
Schedule 5 binds materially distinct runtime configurations to separate applicability and conformance assessment.
No claim of external vendor implementation or Live Voice corpus access is made.

runtime_conformance.notes: PATCH-0015 is a governance and corpus repair plus a cross-runtime conformance framework. Canonical repository implementation does not itself establish vendor implementation or behavioural conformance in any named runtime; such evidence belongs in runtime-bounded observations and failure records.
